## Small example cross-sectional data set {.unnumbered}


### Description

A subset of data from the SEES data, for examples and testing,
data from Pakistan only.



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>sees_pop_data_pk_100
</code></pre>


### Format



<h4><code>sees_pop_data_pk_100</code></h4>

A <code>pop_data</code> object (from <code>as_pop_data()</code>) with 200 rows and 8 columns:


<dl>
<dt>id</dt><dd>Observation ID
</dd>
<dt>Country</dt><dd>Country where the participant was living
</dd>
<dt>cluster</dt><dd>survey sampling cluster
</dd>
<dt>catchment</dt><dd>survey catchment area
</dd>
<dt>age</dt><dd>participant's age when sampled, in years
</dd>
<dt>antigen_iso</dt><dd>which antigen and isotype are being measured
(data is in long format)
</dd>
<dt>value</dt><dd>concentration of antigen isotype, in ELISA units
</dd>
</dl>




### Source

<a href="https://osf.io/n6cp3">https://osf.io/n6cp3</a>
